expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: TDD-referral-code-api.md exists in outputs/

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Document is ~5009 words (wc -w)

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Problem statement and context' explains the referral program need and existing systems

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section 2 has explicit 'In scope (this TDD)' and 'Out of scope' subsections with multiple items each

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '4. High-level approach' with architecture diagram and core flow

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section '5. Detailed design' covers code generation, redemption flow, activation flow, deep links, data model, fraud, activation consumption

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section '7. Alternatives considered' lists 9 alternatives with explicit 'Why not' rejection rationale (e.g., 'Build into Users service', 'Sequential codes', 'Poll Stripe')

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs / Non-functional requirements section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section '8. Non-functional requirements' with concrete numbers: 99.9% availability, p99 < 200ms, 500 RPS peak, < 5 min lag SLO

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section '9. Risks and failure modes' table with R1-R10 risks, likelihood, impact, and mitigation

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section
expectations.evidence: Section '11. Rollout / migration plan' with 10-week phased rollout from schema deployment to public launch

expectations.text: Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
expectations.evidence: Section 8.6 'Observability' with metrics/logs/traces/alerts, and Section 13 'Operations / runbooks'

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section '14. Open questions' has 12 numbered open questions (provider choice, code expiry, FX, etc.)

expectations.text: Contains no fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems (only placeholders or PRD-mentioned references are acceptable)
expectations.evidence: Only example URL 'https://refr.app.example/r/ABCDEF' which uses .example TLD as placeholder; no fabricated internal company URLs

expectations.text: TDD is structured as an API serving design (mentions endpoints/routes/methods, response shapes, AuthN/Z model)
expectations.evidence: Section '6. API contracts' details GET/POST endpoints with JSON request/response shapes, error codes, JWT/mTLS auth model

expectations.text: Defines a data model / schema for referral codes and redemption events with primary keys and important indexes
expectations.evidence: Section 5.5 provides SQL DDL for referral_codes (PK user_id), referrals (PK id, UNIQUE referred_user_id), referral_events with explicit CREATE INDEX statements

expectations.text: Explicitly addresses idempotency of the redemption call (idempotency key, dedup table, conditional write, or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Section 5.3 uses deterministic idempotency keys 'referral:{referral_id}:referrer' and Billing dedupes server-side; UNIQUE constraint on referred_user_id enforces dedupe at DB level

expectations.text: Addresses fraud / self-referral / multi-account abuse prevention (referenced as design concern, not punted)
expectations.evidence: Section 5.6 'Fraud / abuse controls (v1)' lists 7 concrete controls: self-referral block, rate limits, device/IP/payment fingerprint heuristics, daily cap, kill switch, manual review queue

expectations.text: Defines or constrains what 'activation' means (e.g., first paid subscription start) and the credit-issuance trigger flow
expectations.evidence: Activation defined as 'first paid subscription start' and Section 5.3 details the consumer flow: Billing emits account.activated -> state PENDING->ACTIVATING->CREDITED via Billing credit API

expectations.text: Addresses code-collision / generation strategy (random + uniqueness check, deterministic from user ID, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Section 5.1: random 6-char from 30-char alphabet (7.29e8 combinations), UNIQUE constraint with retry-on-conflict up to 5 times, CSPRNG via secrets.token_urlsafe

expectations.text: Has a concrete latency budget for the API (numbers, not 'fast')
expectations.evidence: Section 8.1 capacity table: GET /referral/me p99 150ms, POST /redeem p99 200ms, GET preview p99 100ms; engineering goal 'p99 < 200ms for the mobile-hot endpoints'

expectations.text: Discusses integration with Stripe and the internal credit ledger, including what happens if the credit write fails
expectations.evidence: Section 5.3 step 7: 'On any failure (4xx not retryable, 5xx retryable) state stays ACTIVATING, message goes back on the queue with backoff; DLQ after N retries'; Section 8.3 degradation modes for Billing API down
